title: SpaceX vs the TRUMP coin
question: Is the SpaceX IPO the stock-market version of the TRUMP coin launch — a record retail-mania event that drains its host market and marks the top?
finding: No on the market mechanism, conditional yes on timing, yes at the asset level. The TRUMP launch topped its host market within three days (SOL ATH +2d, BTC ATH +3d) because it was huge relative to its host: ~54% of SOL's market cap, ~2.0% of all crypto, peak-day volume 6.5x chain baseline. SpaceX cannot repeat the drain — $75B is 0.10% of US market cap and 6.8% of one day's traded value — but at the indicated $170 open it would be 3.1% of all US equities, a larger class share than TRUMP's. Mega-IPOs carry late-cycle information: across the 34 largest US-listed IPOs since 1996, median NASDAQ forward 12m return was +4.9% vs +16.0% unconditional (bootstrap p~0.008, overlap-flattered), 59% saw a 20%+ drawdown within a year vs 36% base, a hot-tape base rate (+14.7%) fails as the rival, and the effect halves ex-2000 — record deals cluster into the hottest windows. GameStop is the control: at 0.05% of US equities even 140%-of-float short interest only dented the S&P -3.3% for nine sessions, but the stock fell 88% in 16 sessions once supply normalized. TRUMP, GME and SpaceX share the low-float architecture; SpaceX's float (4.25%) grows 9.4x by day 180 on study 24's calendar — the scarcity premium has an expiry date.
